data.title: American Airlines Drops 1.18%: Fed's Policy Review Shakes Market
"Tech Stocks Tumble as Economic Fears Mount; Nvidia Earnings Loom"
data.description:
Stocks opened in the red on Tuesday, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite indexes down 0.4% and 1.2% respectively at 2 pm ET. Big Tech and crypto stocks were not immune, with the Bloomberg Mag7 Index, which equally weights seven of the largest companies, down almost 3% midway through the session. Nvidia, on the eve of reporting its fourth quarter earnings, was down 1.9% at 2 pm ET. Coinbase and MicroStrategy were down 7.2% and 12.1% respectively at that time.

The decline in stocks can be attributed to two main factors: investor concerns about economic growth and the potential resumption of previously paused tariff plans. A report by The Conference Board showed that consumer confidence in February fell by the most since August 2021, with a reading below 100 indicating a pessimistic economic outlook. Additionally, President Trump stated that levies against Mexico and Canada are "on schedule," signaling a potential resumption of tariff plans that were previously paused.

Looking ahead, the big market movers this week will be Nvidia's earnings report and the release of Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) data. Nvidia, once considered the golden child of AI, has faced headwinds this year, with DeepSeek, a Chinese AI firm, spooking investors and pulling almost $600 billion out of Nvidia last month. However, Nvidia has been able to pare most of the losses, and shares were down only 6% midway through Tuesday's session.

Option chain data suggests that many traders remain optimistic about Nvidia's prospects, with calls hitting $145 to $160. Shares were trading at $128 at 2 pm ET. Nicholas Colas, co-founder of DataTrek Research, advises keeping an eye on the CBOE Volatility Index (the VIX) for indications of future stock moves. A VIX value at or above 27.3 indicates heightened volatility, and historically, stocks tend to perform well after volatility spikes. The VIX is currently hovering around 19, but a bigger selloff in big tech or disappointing inflation data could send the VIX higher.

data.title: Trump's Return: A Mixed Bag for Climate Policy
data.description:
Former U.S. President Donald Trump's return to the political scene has sparked concerns about the potential impact on climate policy, with some analysts suggesting that his influence could be beneficial in certain aspects. As Trump's second term in office looms, there is a growing expectation that his administration will adopt a more skeptical stance towards green subsidies and global climate agreements.

Trump's presidency was marked by a series of decisions that rolled back environmental regulations and withdrew the U.S. from the Paris Agreement, a global pact aimed at reducing greenhouse gas emissions. His administration also supported the fossil fuel industry, including coal, oil, and natural gas, and sought to weaken regulations aimed at protecting the environment.

However, some experts argue that Trump's skepticism towards climate policies could have unintended positive consequences. For instance, his administration's withdrawal from the Paris Agreement may have encouraged other countries to take more ambitious action on climate change. Additionally, Trump's focus on economic growth and job creation could lead to the development of new, clean energy technologies that are more competitive and efficient than existing ones.

Moreover, Trump's return to the political stage could also encourage bipartisan cooperation on climate policy. During his presidency, Trump appointed several Republican lawmakers who were more open to climate action, such as former Secretary of State Rex Tillerson and former Environmental Protection Agency Administrator Scott Pruitt. These appointments suggest that there may be room for compromise and collaboration between Republicans and Democrats on climate policy.

Furthermore, Trump's focus on energy independence and domestic production could also have positive implications for climate policy. By promoting the development of domestic energy sources, such as natural gas and renewable energy, Trump's administration could help reduce the U.S.'s reliance on foreign oil imports, thereby reducing greenhouse gas emissions associated with transportation.

In conclusion, while Trump's return to the political scene has raised concerns about the potential impact on climate policy, some analysts argue that his influence could have unintended positive consequences. His skepticism towards climate policies may encourage other countries to take more ambitious action, promote the development of new clean energy technologies, and foster bipartisan cooperation on climate policy. Additionally, Trump's focus on energy independence and domestic production could also have positive implications for climate policy. However, it remains to be seen how Trump's influence will ultimately shape U.S. climate policy in the coming years.

data.description:
Gold has reached a new record high, but this does not mean there are no more big opportunities correlated with this precious metal. In our view, the next big deal is hiding in plain sight - the stock market, because this company is truly poised to capitalize on this golden momentum and prove itself to be the perfect bet in a shifting economic landscape.

HERE ARE OUR PICKS FOR THIS WEEK!

Newmont Corporation (NEM): Gold's Glimmer Amidst Volatility

Current Share Price: $44.04 

Market Capitalization: $49.31B 

Median Target Price: $52.50

Recommendation: Buy

Newmont Corporation (NEM) is a leading gold mining company, renowned for its expansive operations across multiple continents, significantly influencing the global supply of this precious metal.

Financially, Newmont stands robust with total revenue reaching $18.68 billion, reflecting a remarkable revenue growth of 42.8%. Gross profits are substantial at $9.39 billion, yielding a gross margin of 50.27%, while the EBITDA margins stand at an impressive 46.71%, demonstrating high operational efficiency. The operating margins are also strong at 40.85%.

Newmont's liquidity position is noteworthy, with total cash reserves of $3.64 billion ($3.21 per share) and a formidable operating cash flow of $6.36 billion. However, the company carries a significant debt load, with total debt amounting to $8.97 billion, resulting in a debt-to-equity ratio of 29.80%.

The company showcases positive profitability metrics with a return on assets (ROA) of 6.61% and a return on equity (ROE) of 11.17%, highlighting effective asset and equity management. Profit margins are strong at 17.92%, signaling an effective cost structure and profitability.

Analysts recommend a Buy rating with a mean score of 1.95. Price targets range from $45.00 to $65.43, with the median target set at $52.50. The market sentiment is positive about Newmont's ability to navigate market volatility and continue delivering strong financial performance.

Newmont Corporation offers a stable investment opportunity in the often volatile mining sector, fortified by its robust financials, operational efficiency, and significant market position, making it an attractive choice for investors seeking exposure to precious metals.

FactSet Research Systems Inc. (FDS): Data Powerhouse in Financial Analytics

Current Share Price: $461.56 

Market Capitalization: $17.50B

FactSet Research Systems Inc. (FDS) is a premier provider of financial data and analytics, servicing global investment professionals with comprehensive data solutions integrated with advanced technology.

Financially, FactSet exhibits steady performance with total revenue reaching $2.23 billion, reflecting a revenue growth of 4.9%. Gross profits stand at $1.21 billion, translating to a gross margin of 54.29%, while the EBITDA margins are substantial at 38.03%, demonstrating effective operational efficiency. The operating margins are strong at 34.25%.

FactSet maintains a solid liquidity position with total cash reserves of $358.79 million ($9.43 per share) and a healthy operating cash flow of $631.57 million. The company holds moderate debt, with total debt amounting to $1.55 billion, resulting in a debt-to-equity ratio of 77.93%.

The company showcases commendable profitability metrics with a return on assets (ROA) of 11.16% and a return on equity (ROE) of 29.17%, indicating effective management of its assets and equity. Profit margins are robust at 24.16%, highlighting the strong profitability inherent in its business model.

Analysts have issued a Hold recommendation with a mean rating of 3.29. Price targets vary from $390.00 to $536.00, with the median target set at $486.00. The market sentiment reflects cautious optimism towards FactSet's continued growth amidst market challenges.

FactSet Research Systems Inc. remains a formidable player in the financial analytics sector, supported by steady revenue growth, strong profitability, and a well-structured financial position. It is a viable option for investors seeking exposure to the financial data and analytics market.

data.title: Nvidia Q4 Preview: No Panic! DeepSeek Sparks China's AI Chip Boom
data.description:
On February 26, after U.S. markets close, AI leader NVIDIA will report its latest earnings, marking the grand finale of the Q4 earnings season. 

Recently, DeepSeek's emergence has shaken the AI landscape by achieving results comparable to ChatGPT at a fraction of the cost. This has sparked market concerns over whether demand for GPUs will remain as strong as before. NVIDIA's stock saw a brief tumble but has since recovered its losses. However, Nvidia's stock has fallen nearly 10% YTD, underperforming the S&P 500 and losing its leading position.

Data Center Growth: Can It Withstand DeepSeek's Disruption?

The market's primary focus is NVIDIA's data center business—whether it can maintain its high growth trajectory. Earnings reports from six other Magnificent Seven tech giants indicate that capital expenditures remain robust, suggesting strong GPU demand.

Beyond tech giants, AI startups are also driving demand. Elon Musk's xAI, for example, has launched the Grok 3 model, which runs on 200,000 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, with plans to scale up to 1 million GPUs in the future.

Interestingly, DeepSeek's rise may not necessarily be a negative for NVIDIA. According to Reuters, citing six industry insiders, demand for low-cost AI models like DeepSeek has led to a surge in purchases of NVIDIA's H20 chips, which are designed specifically for China due to U.S. export controls. Moreover, smaller companies in sectors like healthcare and education are now buying AI servers with DeepSeek models and NVIDIA H20 chips—previously a privilege of deep-pocketed financial and telecom firms. Alibaba has also announced that its capital expenditure over the next three years will exceed the past decade's total. With U.S.-China relations showing signs of thawing, potential easing of export controls could further benefit NVIDIA's business in China.

Blackwell GPU: Market Demand in Focus

NVIDIA's next-generation Blackwell GPU is now in production, and market expectations are sky-high. During the earnings call, CEO Jensen Huang's comments on Blackwell's demand will be a critical focus.

Wedbush analysts report that despite DeepSeek's disruptive potential, their surveys indicate that demand for NVIDIA's Blackwell GPUs still far exceeds supply.

UBS forecasts that NVIDIA's Blackwell revenue will hit $9 billion in Q4 2024, well above the expected $5 billion, and expects Q1 2025 revenue to exceed $20 billion with 700,000 units shipped.

Key Partners Reinforce Bullish Sentiment

The strong performance of NVIDIA's key partners, such as TSMC and Super Micro Computer, further supports the bullish case for NVIDIA.

Reports suggest that NVIDIA has secured over 70% of TSMC's CoWoS-L advanced packaging capacity for 2025 to meet demand for Blackwell GPUs. Analysts estimate that NVIDIA's Blackwell chip shipments will grow over 20% per quarter, with annual shipments surpassing 2 million units.

On February 5, Super Micro Computer announced that data center systems featuring NVIDIA's Blackwell GPUs are ready for delivery, alleviating concerns about potential supply chain constraints.

data.title: GO2bank Review (2025): A Mobile Bank with High Savings Rates, Free ATMs, and More
data.description:
GO2bank, a mobile-first banking platform launched by Green Dot Corporation, has been making waves in the digital banking landscape since its inception in 2020. As of 2025, GO2bank offers a compelling suite of features that cater to the needs of everyday people, from high-yield savings accounts to free ATMs and overdraft protection. In this review, we will delve into the key features and benefits of GO2bank, as well as its potential drawbacks and how it compares to traditional banks.



High-Yield Savings Account

GO2bank's high-yield savings account offers an interest rate of 4.50% APY, which is significantly higher than the national average savings rate. This competitive rate allows users to grow their savings more quickly, making it an attractive option for those looking to maximize their earnings. Additionally, GO2bank's savings account has no monthly fees with eligible direct deposit, otherwise $5 per month, and no ATM fees in their free nationwide ATM network. These features make it an appealing choice for those seeking a low-cost, high-yield savings account.

Free ATMs

GO2bank offers free access to thousands of ATMs nationwide, allowing users to withdraw cash without incurring fees. This extensive network ensures that users can easily find an in-network ATM, even in rural areas. GO2bank also reimburses users for out-of-network ATM fees, up to $3 per withdrawal, plus any additional fees the ATM owner or bank may charge. This feature makes it convenient for users to access their cash when they need it, without worrying about hidden fees.

Overdraft Protection

GO2bank offers up to $200 in overdraft protection for qualified users, helping them avoid costly overdraft fees. This feature is particularly useful for those who may occasionally overspend or have an unexpected expense. However, it is essential to note that GO2bank's overdraft protection is limited to debit card purchase transactions and is subject to certain criteria, such as having an activated chip-enabled debit card and maintaining eligible direct deposits. Additionally, GO2bank reserves the right to not pay overdrafts, even if the user meets all the criteria.



Early Direct Deposit Availability

GO2bank offers early direct deposit availability, allowing users to access their funds up to 2 days early for paychecks and up to 4 days early for government benefits. This feature can help users manage their cash flow more effectively, as they can access their funds before their payday or benefit payment date.

Secured Credit Card

GO2bank offers a secured credit card that reports to all three major credit bureaus, helping users build their credit history. This card has no annual fee and includes free credit score monitoring. It is an excellent option for those looking to establish or rebuild their credit.

Bill Pay and Budgeting Tools

GO2bank's mobile app includes bill pay and budgeting tools, allowing users to manage their finances more efficiently. These features enable users to track their spending, plan for future expenses, and automate bill payments.

Customer Support

GO2bank offers customer support through its mobile app and website, as well as via phone. Users can connect with support representatives via chat or email, and the app also offers 24/7 live chat support. While GO2bank's customer support is primarily online, users may find it limiting compared to traditional banks with more extensive in-person service options.



Potential Drawbacks

While GO2bank offers numerous benefits, there are also some potential drawbacks to consider:

1. Lack of Physical Locations: As a mobile-only bank, GO2bank has no physical branches. This can make it difficult for users who prefer in-person assistance or need to access cash or deposit checks in person.
2. Technical Issues: Relying on a mobile app for banking services can be subject to technical issues, such as app crashes, slow internet connections, or even app-wide outages. These issues can make it difficult for users to access their accounts or complete transactions.
3. Security Concerns: While GO2bank emphasizes security features like two-factor authentication and fraud alerts, there is still a risk of unauthorized access to user accounts through mobile devices. Users should be cautious about protecting their mobile devices and personal information.
4. Limited Customer Support: GO2bank's customer support is primarily online, which can make it more challenging for users to get help with complex issues or when they need immediate assistance. Additionally, users may face longer wait times during peak hours or when trying to reach support through the app or website.

Conclusion

GO2bank's mobile-first approach to banking offers a compelling suite of features, including a high-yield savings account, free ATMs, overdraft protection, and early direct deposit availability. Its secured credit card and bill pay tools further enhance its appeal for users seeking a convenient, low-cost banking solution. However, potential drawbacks such as the lack of physical locations, technical issues, security concerns, and limited customer support should be considered. Ultimately, GO2bank's offerings make it an attractive option for those looking for a mobile banking alternative with competitive interest rates and low fees.

data.title: Nebius Challenges Hyperscalers With Dedicated AI Computing Services, Transparent Pricing: Analyst
data.description:
Nebius Group N.V. (NASDAQ:NBIS) is making waves in the cloud computing landscape by positioning itself as a key alternative to hyperscalers like AWS, Azure, and GCP. The company's vertically integrated model and ownership of data centers provide a structural advantage over hyperscalers in handling next-generation AI needs. D.A. Davidson & Co. analyst Alexander Platt initiated coverage on Nebius with a Buy rating and a price forecast of $50, highlighting the company's unique value proposition.

Nebius' vertically integrated model and ownership of data centers enable it to offer dedicated AI computing services with transparent pricing and scalable infrastructure tailored for AI workloads. This approach addresses the needs of AI startups and enterprises by providing a more cost-effective and efficient alternative to the high premiums often charged by hyperscalers. Nebius' ability to scale its infrastructure efficiently and maintain lower energy costs through data center ownership further enhances its competitive edge.

Nebius caters to specific AI workloads such as AI model training, AI inference, and AI-optimized services. Its scalable infrastructure tailored for AI workloads enables AI startups and enterprises to handle varying workload demands efficiently and cost-effectively. By offering dedicated AI computing services with transparent pricing, Nebius allows AI-focused businesses to focus on their core competencies without worrying about the underlying infrastructure.



Nebius' transparent pricing strategy is a significant advantage over hyperscalers, which leverage rigid pricing and supply constraints to secure high margins. By offering competitive pricing and handling long-term AI workloads, Nebius is uniquely positioned to capture market share in the growing AI computing landscape. As AI adoption grows, companies will need scalable, cost-effective compute solutions, which hyperscalers often fail to provide without high premiums.

Its scalable infrastructure tailored for AI workloads addresses the needs of AI startups and enterprises by offering a more cost-effective and efficient alternative to the high premiums often charged by hyperscalers. Nebius' transparent pricing strategy is a significant advantage over hyperscalers, which leverage rigid pricing and supply constraints to secure high margins. As AI adoption grows, Nebius is well-positioned to capture market share in the expanding AI computing landscape.

data.title: GameStop Urged to Adopt Bitcoin Standard for Financial Turnaround
data.description:
GameStop, the struggling video game retailer, has been urged by Strive Asset Management to adopt the Bitcoin Standard as a means to transform its financial future. In a letter to GameStop's Chairman and CEO, Ryan Cohen, Strive's CEO, Matt Cole, outlined a strategy that involves shifting the company's cash reserves from a shrinking asset to a growth engine.

Strive Asset Management, which holds shares of GameStop through its exchange-traded funds (ETFs), sees potential for GameStop to become a premier Bitcoin treasury company in the gaming sector. With nearly $5 billion in cash on its balance sheet, GameStop is in a unique position to make this shift. Cole noted that GameStop has reduced its operating losses over the past two years and offset these deficits with interest income from cash holdings generated from equity offerings, stabilizing the company's balance sheet and positioning it for more dynamic strategic moves.

However, Cole acknowledged that GameStop's core challenge remains: brick-and-mortar retail is in decline as shifting consumer preferences favor digital downloads of games and content. To address this, Cole suggests that GameStop should make Bitcoin the hurdle rate for capital deployment. He proposes that the company should allocate the maximum portion of cash reserves to Bitcoin, which he sees as a true savings asset over the long run and a protection against inflationary pressures affecting fiat currencies.

Cole advises against allocating treasury resources to other cryptocurrencies, which he believes could dilute shareholder value and potentially impair GameStop's ability to raise future financing. To further reduce GameStop's retail footprint, Cole suggests closing additional stores, focusing on online platforms, and cutting costs until operating at a profit. He also proposes leveraging capital markets to fund additional Bitcoin purchases through at-the-market (ATM) offerings and convertible debt securities.

In the future, Cole suggests that GameStop should strategically deploy Bitcoin reserves for strategic growth initiatives and M&A opportunities in the gaming sector when the long-term expected rate of return exceeds simply holding Bitcoin. By adopting a treasury strategy centered on adopting the Bitcoin Standard, Cole believes that GameStop could move beyond "meme" sentiment, forging a stable foundation to drive sustainable shareholder value.

data.title: Grayscale Pushes for Cardano and XRP ETFs Amidst Growing Crypto Demand
data.description:
Grayscale Seeks SEC Approval for Cardano Fund and XRP Trust ETF Conversion Amid Growing Interest in Crypto Products

Grayscale Investments, a prominent player in the crypto asset management space, is expanding its offerings with new ETF proposals. The company has recently sought approval from the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) for a Cardano fund and the conversion of its XRP Trust into an ETF. These developments come amidst a growing appetite from investors seeking new avenues for crypto exposure.

The SEC plays a pivotal role in the approval processes of crypto-related financial products. Grayscale's endeavor to convert its XRP Trust into an ETF follows a trend in which other crypto fund issuers, like 21Shares, are making similar attempts. The SEC's willingness to consider regulatory frameworks that address the complexities of trading digital assets is evident in its move to publish details of NYSE Arca's proposal for the XRP Trust.

The success of spot Bitcoin funds has set a significant precedent in the market. Recent reports indicate that these funds have attracted over $40 billion in net inflows, with BlackRock's Bitcoin Trust boasting approximately $55 billion in assets under management. This surge suggests increased institutional interest in digital assets, positioning them as viable investment alternatives.

As Grayscale moves to introduce its Polkadot ETF, it's essential to examine current market trends. Polkadot, known for its ability to connect various blockchain networks, has seen mixed performance recently, trading at $4.42—a decline of 6.5% within 24 hours. This trend reflects broader losses across major digital assets, emphasizing the volatility characteristic of the crypto market and the potential challenges for new fund entrants.

Industry analysts are closely watching these developments. Bloomberg Senior Analyst Eric Balchunas mentioned that the recent Polkadot filings indicate a favorable shift in the SEC's stance towards crypto products. He noted, "The floodgates were going to be open anyway... but now the floodgates are fully open." His insights suggest a growing optimism among ETF issuers as regulatory hurdles appear less daunting.

The journey towards the establishment of more crypto ETFs is gaining momentum, and Grayscale's initiatives exemplify this trend. As more assets like Solana are considered for ETFs, the likelihood of successful approvals seems promising. Bal

data.title: Dynamic Dividends Surge: Abrdn's 1.08% Intraday Boost
Super Group's 2025 Earnings: A 5.94% Plunge!
Graphs Worth 1000 Words: U.S Stocks Lose Steam as China Tech Takes Lead
data.description:
A Graph is worth a 1,000 words—let's take a look at some intriguing charts!

The unstoppable rally in U.S. stocks seems to be losing steam this year. The three major U.S. indices have posted only single-digit gains, underperforming both Chinese and European assets.

Adding to concerns, insider selling in U.S. stocks has been rising steadily, signaling that corporate executives are cashing out aggressively.

The 10 largest U.S. stock ETFs have recently seen a sharp outflow of funds, indicating that investors may be rotating out of U.S. equities.

Meanwhile, Chinese internet ETFs listed in the U.S. have recorded strong inflows. Led by Alibaba, Chinese tech stocks are gaining traction and may soon take over the leadership role previously held by the Magnificent 7.

Macro Outlook: Inflation Fears & Fed Policy

On the macro front, long-term U.S. inflation expectations for the next 5–10 years have climbed to a 30-year high, driven by Trump's aggressive tariff policies. The hotter-than-expected January CPI report has also cast doubt on Fed rate cuts this year.

Signs of Recovery in China's Real Estate Market

China's real estate sector is showing early signs of stabilization and recovery. Developers are once again bidding aggressively for land, reflecting a return of confidence in the property market.

According to China Index Academy, 37% of land transactions worth over ¥1 billion(approximate $0.138 Bln) this year were sold at premiums exceeding 20%. This marks a significant rebound from just 14% in 2024 and under 5% in 2023. For comparison, during the real estate boom of 2017, this figure surpassed 60%!

Notably, over 60% of developers purchasing land at premiums are state-backed enterprises (SOBs). With major private developers like Evergrande and Country Garden facing severe financial distress, SOBs have stepped in decisively to stabilize market confidence. Since the property downturn began in 2021, SOBs have consistently accounted for more than 50% of premium land acquisitions.

data.title: DeepSeek Looks To Launch Its New AI Model Earlier Than Expected
data.description:
According to the media report, DeepSeek is planning to accelerate the release of its next-generation AI model, R2.

Sources familiar with the matter revealed that R2 was originally scheduled for release in early May, but the company now aims to launch it sooner. DeepSeek stated that the new model will feature improvements in programming capabilities and multilingual reasoning.

Vijayasimha Alilughatta, Chief Operating Officer of Indian tech service provider Zensar, believes that "The launch of DeepSeek's R2 model could be a pivotal moment in the AI industry." He added that its cost-effective approach to AI model development "would likely spur companies worldwide to accelerate their own efforts ... breaking the stranglehold of the few dominant players in the field."

Analysts attribute DeepSeek's success to over a decade of substantial investment in research and computing power by its backer, quantitative hedge fund "High-Flyer". Between 2020 and 2021, Huanfang invested 1.2 billion yuan to establish two AI supercomputing clusters, which include approximately 10,000 Nvidia A100 chips.

Former employees of DeepSeek noted that the company's success is largely due to founder Liang Wenfeng's focus on more cost-effective AI architectures. DeepSeek has adopted technologies such as Mixture of Experts (MoE) and Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA), significantly reducing computational costs.

The report also highlighted DeepSeek's flat management structure, which fosters a collaborative atmosphere. Founder Liang Wenfeng, described by former colleagues as low-key and introverted, prefers to dive into technical details alongside younger employees.

A former employee quoted in the report said, Liang gave us control and treated us as experts. He constantly asked questions and learned alongside us.

In general, DeepSeek looks to challenge industry giants and inspire global enterprises to accelerate their AI efforts once again, and this time, it may aim to completely shape the future of the AI industry.

Harmony Biosciences Q4 2024: Key Contradictions in ZYN002 Study, Trial Enrollment, and Market Dynamics
data.description:
These are the key contradictions discussed in Harmony Biosciences' latest 2024Q4 earnings call, specifically including: ZYN002 Phase 3 Study Design and Primary Endpoint, Impact of Competition on Pitolisant HD Trial Enrollment, and Pediatric Narcolepsy Patient Population and Seasonality Impact:



Record Financial Performance:
- Harmony Bioscience reported net product revenues of $714.7 million for 2024, representing a 23% growth year-over-year, with $201.3 million in net revenue for Q4.
- This growth was driven by the strong performance of WAKIX in narcolepsy and the company's strategic acquisitions.

Pipeline Advancements:
- The company's pipeline includes three orphan rare neurology franchises, each with potential peak sales opportunities of $1 billion to $2 billion.
- These advancements are supported by a robust late-stage CNS pipeline and strategic acquisitions, positioning Harmony for long-term growth.

Commercial and Market Positioning:
- WAKIX generated $201.3 million in net revenue for Q4, contributing to a 23% increase year-over-year, with an average of 7,100 patients on the drug.
- This growth is due to WAKIX's broad clinical utility and differentiated profile, along with effective commercial execution.

Intellectual Property and Legal Outcomes:
- Harmony Biosciences reached a settlement with Novagen Pharma, granting them a license to sell a generic version of Pitolisant beginning January 2030 or earlier.
- The settlement reinforces the strength of Harmony's intellectual property portfolio and commitment to defending it.

data.title: AZ Boom: Willis Towers Watson Surges 1.19%!
